source: ExchangeRate-API (open.er-api.com) mid-market rates 22:30 UTC, cross-referenced with Wise and Trading Economics
baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↑ ZMW = Kwacha weakening (depreciating).
mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate held at 13.50% — reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held rate at May 2026 review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: ExchangeRate-API Jun 23: 1 USD = 17.889 ZMW (1 ZMW = 0.0559 USD). Kwacha slightly softer vs USD from 17.824 (Jun 19) — 0.4% depreciation over the long weekend.

GBP.note: 1 GBP = 23.641 ZMW (ExchangeRate-API Jun 23). Sterling slightly firmer vs kwacha from 23.569 (Jun 19).

EUR.note: 1 EUR = 20.492 ZMW (ExchangeRate-API Jun 23). Euro slightly firmer vs kwacha from 20.446 (Jun 19).

ZAR.note: 1 ZAR = 1.089 ZMW (ExchangeRate-API Jun 23). Rand slightly firmer vs kwacha from 1.085 (Jun 19). 1 ZMW = 0.918 ZAR.

BWP.note: Botswana pula. ExchangeRate-API cross: 1 ZMW = 0.787 BWP.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. ExchangeRate-API: 1 ZMW = 0.379 CNY → 1 CNY = 2.638 ZMW.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha slightly softer across the board after the long weekend — moderate USD strength continuing to drive cross-rates. Kwacha weakening 0.2–0.4% vs major pairs since Jun 19.
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: Kwacha showing mild weakness after profit-taking from Jun 11 peak (17.46). The long weekend saw some USD demand build. Overall range-bound in 17.80–18.00 area. Copper prices and IMF programme progress remain key catalysts. BOZ likely to lean against excessive weakness to manage inflation expectations.
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Expect continued appreciation bias but with increased volatility ahead of August 2026 elections.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Post-election clarity determines direction — policy continuity supports kwacha, fiscal slippage reverses gains.
